2605.18309.title: Alignment Dynamics in LLM Fine-Tuning
2605.18309.paper_summary: Although Large Language Models (LLMs) achieve strong alignment through supervised fine-tuning and reinforcement learning from human feedback, the alignment is often fragile under subsequent fine-tuning. Existing explanations either attribute alignment fragility to gradient geometry or characterize it as a distributional shift in model outputs, yet few provide a unified account that bridges parameter-space learning dynamics with function-space alignment behavior during fine-tuning. In this work, we introduce a tractable alignment score and derive its closed-form update during fine-tuning, yielding a unified framework for alignment dynamics. Our analysis decomposes alignment updates into two competing components: a \textbf{\color{red!60!black} Rebound Force}, governed jointly by the current alignment state and the narrowness of model distribution, and a \textbf{\color{green!60!black} Driving Force}, determined by how the training distribution aligns with outcome-conditioned posteriors over aligned and non-aligned completions. This decomposition explains why prior alignment can be reversed by later fine-tuning and why narrower posterior structure strengthens such reversal. Moreover, our framework predicts a \textbf{Rehearsal Priming Effect}: prior alignment leaves a latent posterior imprint that amplifies the effective Driving Force upon re-exposure, leading to faster re-alignment. We validate these predictions across safety alignment, emergent misalignment, and sentiment settings, demonstrating consistent alignment reversal and accelerated re-alignment under re-exposure. In addition, controlled experiments in safety alignment confirm the predicted dependence of rebound strength on posterior narrowness. Together, these results provide a unified dynamical perspective on how alignment is disrupted and reactivated during LLM fine-tuning.
2605.18309.paper_summary_zh: 儘管大型語言模型（LLMs）通過監督微調和從人類反饋中進行強化學習實現了強大的對齊，但在隨後的微調中，這種對齊往往是脆弱的。現有的解釋要麼將對齊脆弱性歸因於梯度幾何，要麼將其描述為模型輸出中的分佈變化，但很少有提供統一解釋的研究，將參數空間學習動態與微調過程中的函數空間對齊行為聯繫起來。在這項工作中，我們引入了一個可處理的對齊分數，並推導出其在微調過程中的封閉形式更新，從而產生了一個統一的對齊動態框架。我們的分析將對齊更新分解為兩個相互競爭的組件：一個是由當前對齊狀態和模型分佈的狹窄性共同決定的 \textbf{\color{red!60!black} 反彈力}，另一個是由訓練分佈與基於結果的後驗在對齊和非對齊完成之間的對齊程度決定的 \textbf{\color{green!60!black} 驅動力}。這一分解解釋了為什麼先前的對齊可以被後來的微調逆轉，以及為什麼更狹窄的後驗結構會加強這種逆轉。此外，我們的框架預測了一個 \textbf{重複啟動效應}：先前的對齊留下潛在的後驗印記，這在重新暴露時放大了有效的驅動力，導致更快的重新對齊。我們在安全對齊、新興錯位和情感設置中驗證了這些預測，展示了在重新暴露下持續的對齊逆轉和加速的重新對齊。此外，在安全對齊中的控制實驗確認了反彈強度對後驗狹窄性的預測依賴性。總體而言，這些結果提供了對於大型語言模型微調過程中，對齊如何被破壞和重新激活的統一動態視角。
2605.18309.author: Yuhan Huang et.al.
2605.18309.authors: Yuhan Huang, Huanran Chen, Yinpeng Dong
